expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: modern grid hyperlink-style coverage separates contextual-link styling from ordinary element rendering
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly states the case focus and builds required Phase 4a coverage around separating linked vs non-linked styling: "Case focus: modern grid hyperlink-style coverage separates contextual-link styling from ordinary element rendering" and "This implies Phase 4a scenario coverage must explicitly test: 1) Contextual-link styling... and 2) Non-contextual/ordinary rendering... ensuring the styling does not leak." It further includes separate subcategories for "contextual link affordance" vs "ordinary (non-contextual) styling" and a "Mixed state" check.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: result.md is framed as a Phase 4a advisory contract coverage check and constrains the output to Phase 4a structure: "Primary phase under test: phase4a (subcategory-only draft writer)" and "The Phase 4a draft must remain subcategory-first (no top-level canonical groupings like 'EndToEnd', 'Compatibility', etc.)." It also states: "The required coverage can be expressed entirely as Phase 4a subcategories and scenarios."
